events.label: reasoning
events.text:
We need answer JSON. Need analyze schedule. Let's parse image. Activities with durations and predecessors. Early-start bars. Disruption: Activity P (Flooring) needs 2 extra workdays — material delivery slipped. Need project finish delay in workdays, whether completion slips, what drives answer.

We need compute original project duration and new duration after P duration increases from 1d to 3d. Determine if P is on critical path. Let's reconstruct schedule.

Activities:
A Mobilize & layout [3d] no pred (starts 0-3)
B Underground rough-in [2d] pred A (starts 3-5)
C Slab pour [2d] pred B (5-7)
CC Slab cure [3d] pred C (7-10) hatched lag cannot compress
D Framing [5d] pred CC (10-15)
E Roof dry-in [3d] pred D (15-18)
F Electrical rough [3d] pred E (18-21)
G Plumbing top-out [3d] pred E (18-21)
H HVAC rough [3d] pred E (18-21)
I Rough inspection [1d] pred F,G,H (21-22)
J Insulation [4d] pred I (22-26)
K Insulation inspection [1d] pred J (26-27)
L Drywall hang [2d] pred K (27-29)
M Tape & finish [4d] pred L (29-33)
N Paint [3d] pred M (33-36)
O Trim carpentry [3d] pred N (36-39)
P Flooring [1d] pred N (36-37)
Q Final inspection [1d] pred O,P (39-40)

Original finish workday 40. P is parallel to O, shorter. O drives Q. P has float. P duration 1, O duration 3. P starts at N finish 36, finishes 37. O finishes 39. Q starts 39. If P increases by 2 to 3 days, P starts 36 finishes 39, same as O. Q still starts 39. No project delay. Value 0. Completion date does not slip. Driver: P has 2 days of total float (or O is critical). Need identify what drives answer: O (Trim carpentry) is critical path through final; P has float equal to disruption, so absorbed.

Need output JSON with required fields. Many fields not applicable. decision? This is schedule impact assessment, not pass/fail of work quality. The task says decide what work can proceed, blocked, recovery plan valid. We can set decision "pass"? Maybe because no delay? But decision likely pass/fail/needs_more_info for work condition. Since no dangerous/non-compliant, decision pass. risk low. work_stage in-progress. percent_complete maybe estimate overall progress? The stage is in-progress; percent_complete numeric estimate. We can estimate from schedule maybe around 85-90%? But not asked. Could set 0 if unknown? The schema says percent_complete is numeric estimate of overall progress. We can estimate based on current time? The disruption note at bottom, stage in-progress. But no observed date. Maybe percent_complete unknown; set 0? Better set maybe 85? Hmm. The task: "Read the schedule and the disruption from the image. Report the project finish delay in workdays in the value field (0 if the float absorbs it), state whether the completion date slips, and identify what drives your answer." So many JSON fields are generic. We need fill reasonably.

workable: list activity IDs that can still start. Since P delayed by material delivery, P cannot start until materials arrive? Actually P needs 2 extra workdays — material delivery slipped. It may still start but take longer. The question asks what work can proceed, blocked, recovery plan. Workable activities not on hold? At current stage near end? We don't know current date. Could list activities not dependent on P and not completed? Hard. Maybe leave empty? Schema says use workable for list of activity IDs when task asks what work can still start. It does ask "decide what work can proceed, what is blocked". So we should provide workable list. But we need infer from schedule. Since disruption is P only, all other activities can proceed as scheduled except P may be delayed if materials not there. But P is in-progress? The note says needs 2 extra workdays — material delivery slipped. That implies P is delayed/blocked until materials arrive, or its duration extends. Work that can proceed: O (Trim carpentry) can proceed because independent of P and on critical path. Q cannot proceed until both O and P done. P blocked. N maybe complete? We don't kn…
